topics.title: Pancreatic Nets
topics.html:
[!info]
pNETs = Neuroendocrine Tumours of Pancreatic Origin. 2nd Most Common Functional NET (After Carcinoid). Functional (Hormone-Secreting) vs Non-Functional. Insulinoma (Most Common Functional) > Gastrinoma > Glucagonoma > VIPoma > Somatostatinoma. Non-Functional = Majority (60-90%). MEN1 Association (Multiple, Higher Malignant Potential).

1. Learning Objectives

By the end of this note you should be able to:

  • [ ] Classify pNETs by functionality and grade
  • [ ] Recognise clinical syndromes (Insulinoma, Gastrinoma/ZES, Glucagonoma, VIPoma, Somatostatinoma)
  • [ ] Apply localisation techniques (EUS, DOTATATE PET, CT/MRI)
  • [ ] Apply management algorithms (Surgery vs Medical vs PRRT)
  • [ ] Understand MEN1-associated pNETs

2. Classification

By Functionality

CategoryPercentageExamples
Functional (Hormone-Secreting)10-40%Insulinoma, Gastrinoma, Glucagonoma, VIPoma, Somatostatinoma, PPoma, ACTHoma
Non-Functional60-90%No Clinical Hormone Syndrome; Often Detected Incidentally or by Mass Effect

By Grade (WHO 2019)

GradeKi-67 IndexMitotic Count (per 2mm²)Behaviour
NET G1<3%<2Well-Differentiated, Indolent
NET G23-20%2-20Well-Differentiated, Intermediate
NEC G3>20%>20Poorly Differentiated, Aggressive

3. Functional pNETs — Clinical Syndromes

1. Insulinoma (Most Common Functional pNET)

FeatureDetails
Frequency~40-50% of Functional pNETs
PathophysiologyAutonomous Insulin Secretion → Hypoglycaemia
Whipple's TriadSymptoms of Hypoglycaemia + Plasma Glucose <2.8 mmol/L + Relief with Glucose
ClinicalFasting Hypoglycaemia (Early Morning), Neuroglycopenic (Confusion, Seizures), Adrenergic (Sweating, Tremor)
Diagnosis72h Supervised Fast: Glucose <2.8 mmol/L + Insulin ≥6 mU/L + C-Peptide ≥200 pmol/L + Proinsulin ≥5 pmol/L
LocalisationEUS (Sensitivity 90%), ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PET/CT, MRI/MRCP, Selective Arterial Calcium Stimulation (SACI)
TreatmentSurgical Enucleation (Curative, 90% Benign); Diazoxide (Medical Bridge); Everolimus (Unresectable)

2. Gastrinoma (Zollinger-Ellison Syndrome)

FeatureDetails
Frequency20-30% of Functional pNETs
PathophysiologyGastrin Hypersecretion → ↑ Gastric Acid → Refractory PUD
ClinicalRefractory/Recurrent PUD, Diarrhoea (Acid Inactivation of Lipase), Oesophagitis, Steatorrhoea
DiagnosisFasting Gastrin >1000 pg/mL + Gastric pH <2 (or Secretin Stimulation: Gastrin Rise >120 pg/mL)
LocalisationDuodenal > Pancreatic (MEN1: Duodenal Predominant); ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PET/CT, EUS, Secretin Stimulation Test
MEN1 Association20-25% of Gastrinomas; Duodenal Wall (Multiple, Small)
TreatmentHigh-Dose PPI (Omeprazole 40-80mg BD) + Surgical Resection (Duodenotomy + Enucleation)

3. Glucagonoma

FeatureDetails
Frequency5-10% of Functional pNETs
PathophysiologyGlucagon Hypersecretion → ↑ Hepatic Gluconeogenesis/Glycogenolysis
Clinical TriadNME (Necrolytic Migratory Erythema), Diabetes, Weight Loss
OtherGlossitis, Angular Stomatitis, DVT (Hypercoagulable), Anaemia
DiagnosisFasting Glucagon >500 pg/mL (Normal <50-100)
TreatmentSurgical Resection (Curative); Octreotide (Symptom Control); Zinc (Skin); Insulin (Diabetes)

4. VIPoma (Verner-Morrison Syndrome)

FeatureDetails
Frequency<5% (Rare)
PathophysiologyVIP Hypersecretion → ↑ cAMP → ↑ Intestinal Cl⁻/Water Secretion
ClinicalWDHA Syndrome: Watery Diarrhoea (Profuse, >1L/day), Hypokalaemia, Achlorhydria
DiagnosisVIP >75 pg/mL (Normal <75)
TreatmentSurgical Resection; Octreotide (↓ VIP Secretion); Loperamide, Fluid/Electrolyte Replacement

5. Somatostatinoma

FeatureDetails
Frequency<1% (Rarest)
ClinicalDiabetes (↓ Insulin), Steatorrhoea (↓ Pancreatic Enzymes), Gallstones (↓ CCK), Hypochlorhydria, Weight Loss
DiagnosisSomatostatin >100 pg/mL (Fasting)
AssociationNF1 (Associated), MEN1

6. PPoma (Pancreatic Polypeptide)

FeatureDetails
FrequencyRare
ClinicalOften Asymptomatic; Watery Diarrhoea, Weight Loss
DiagnosisPP >300 pmol/L (Fasting)
TreatmentSurgical Resection if Symptomatic

4. Non-Functional pNETs (NF-pNETs)

FeatureDetails
Prevalence60-90% of pNETs
PresentationIncidental (Imaging), Mass Effect (Pain, Obstruction, Jaundice), Metastatic (Liver)
BiochemistryNormal Hormones (May Have ↑ PP, CgA)
GradingG1/G2 (Well-Diff) vs G3 (Poorly Diff)
TreatmentSurgery if >2cm / Symptomatic / Growing; Observation if <1cm, G1, Asymptomatic

5. Localisation — Imaging Modalities

ModalitySensitivitySpecificityRole
EUS (Endoscopic US)90-95%90%+Best for Small Lesions (<1cm); Guided FNA
⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PET/CT90-95%90%+Gold Standard for Staging (Whole Body, SSTR2+)
MRI/MRCP (Pancreas Protocol)85-90%90%+Anatomic Detail; Ductal Relationship
CT (Multiphasic)80-90%85%+First-Line (Accessibility)
Selective Arterial Calcium Stimulation (SACI)90%+95%+Insulinoma Localisation (If Non-Localising)

6. Management by Subtype

Insulinoma

AspectDetails
SurgeryEnucleation (Curative; 90% Benign)
MedicalDiazoxide (↓ Insulin Release); Everolimus (Unresectable)
LocalisationEUS, SACI (Selective Arterial Calcium Stimulation)

Gastrinoma (ZES)

AspectDetails
SurgeryEnucleation + Duodenotomy (Duodenal Gastrinomas Often Small/Multiple)
MedicalHigh-Dose PPI (Omeprazole 40-80mg BD); Octreotide (Adjunct)
MEN1Duodenal Gastrinomas (Multiple, Small) → Longitudinal Duodenotomy

Glucagonoma

AspectDetails
SurgeryResection (Curative if Localised)
MedicalOctreotide (↓ Glucagon), Insulin (Diabetes), Zinc (Skin), Anticoagulation (DVT Prophylaxis)

VIPoma

AspectDetails
SurgeryResection (Curative if Localised)
MedicalOctreotide (↓ VIP → ↓ Diarrhoea), Loperamide, Fluid/Electrolyte Replacement

Non-Functional pNETs

Size / GradeManagement
<1cm, G1Observation (EUS/MRI q6-12mo)
1-2cm, G1Consider Surgery (Patient Factors)
>2cm or G2/G3Surgical Resection (Enucleation / Partial Pancreatectomy)
MetastaticSST Analogues (Octreotide/Lanreotide) → PRRT (¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE) → Chemo (CAPTEM) → Everolimus / Sunitinib

7. MEN1-Associated pNETs

FeatureMEN1-AssociatedSporadic
MultiplicityMultiple (Often >3)Usually Solitary
GastrinomaDuodenal (Multiple, Small) > PancreaticPancreatic > Duodenal
MultiplicityCommon (Multiple Synchronou)Rare
GeneticsMEN1 MutationSporadic Mutations (MEN1, DAXX/ATRX, MEN1)
SurgeryEnucleation + Duodenotomy (Gastrinoma)Standard Resection

8. Treatment Algorithms

Localised Disease

ScenarioManagement
InsulinomaEnucleation (Laparoscopic)
GastrinomaDuodenotomy + Enucleation (Multiple in MEN1)
Glucagonoma / VIPoma / SomatostatinomaResection (Distal Pancreatectomy if Body/Tail)
Non-Functional >2cm or G2/G3Resection (Distal Pancreatectomy / Enucleation / Whipple)

Metastatic / Unresectable

LineTherapy
1st LineSomatostatin Analogues (Octreotide LAR / Lanreotide) — Symptom + Tumour Control
2nd LineEverolimus (mTOR Inhibitor) — RADIANT-3: PFS 11 vs 4.6mo
2nd LineSunitinib (TKI) — PFS 11.4 vs 5.5mo
3rd LinePRRT (¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE) — NETTER-1: PFS 65% vs 11% (G1/G2)
ChemotherapyCAPTEM (Capecitabine + Temozolomide) — NEC G3 / High-Grade G2

9. PRRT (Peptide Receptor Radionuclide Therapy)

Agent¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE (Lutathera)
IndicationSSTR2+ Progressive NET G1/G2
Protocol7.4 GBq IV q8wk × 4 Cycles + Amino Acid Infusion (Renal Protection)
Efficacy (NETTER-1)PFS 65% vs 11% (Octreotide LAR) at 20mo
EligibilitySSTR2+ (Krenning ≥2), Progressive, G1/G2
ToxicityNausea, Myelosuppression, Renal (Amino Acid Protection)

10. Surveillance & Follow-Up

GradeImagingBiochemicalFrequency
G1Annual CT/MRI + ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PETCgA, Hormone PanelAnnual
G26-Monthly CT/MRICgA, Hormone Panel6-Monthly
G3 (NEC)3-Monthly CTKi-67, Hormone Panel3-Monthly

Post-Operative Surveillance (Post-Resection)

TimepointAssessments
3 MonthsHormones (Specific + CgA), CT/MRI
1 YearFull Panel + MRI/CT
Annual (Years 1-5)Hormones + MRI/CT
Years 5-10q1-2yr

11. Exam Pearls (FCPS/MRCP)

TopicKey Point
pNETs Most Common FunctionalInsulinoma (40-50%) > Gastrinoma > Glucagonoma
InsulinomaWhipple's Triad; Enucleation (Curative, 90% Benign)
GastrinomaZES; Duodenal > Pancreatic in MEN1; Secretin Test (+120 pg/mL)
GlucagonomaNME (Necrolytic Migratory Erythema) + Diabetes + Weight Loss
VIPomaWDHA Syndrome: Watery Diarrhoea, Hypokalaemia, Achlorhydria
SomatostatinomaDiabetes + Steatorrhoea + Gallstones + Hypochlorhydria
Non-Functional pNET60-90%; <1cm G1 → Observe; >2cm or G2/G3 → Resect
LocalisationEUS (90-95%) > ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PET (Staging) > MRI/MRCP
Insulinoma LocalisationEUS (90%), SACI (Selective Arterial Calcium Stimulation)
Gastrinoma in MEN1Duodenal > Pancreatic; Multiple; Duodenotomy + Enucleation
GlucagonomaNME (Necrolytic Migratory Erythema) + Diabetes + Weight Loss
VIPomaWDHA Syndrome (Watery Diarrhoea, Hypokalaemia, Achlorhydria)
PRRT (¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE)NETTER-1: PFS 65% vs 11% (Octreotide)
Everolimus/SunitinibRADIANT-3: Everolimus PFS 11 vs 4.6mo; Sunitinib 11.4 vs 5.5mo
CAPTEMCapecitabine + Temozolomide (NEC G3 / High-Grade G2)
SurveillanceG1: Annual; G2: 6-Monthly; G3: 3-Monthly (CT + CgA + Hormones)

12. Confusions & Mnemonics

ConfusionClarification
Insulinoma vs FactitiousInsulinoma: C-Peptide ↑, Proinsulin ↑; Factitious: C-Peptide Low, Insulin High
Gastrinoma vs ZESZES = Gastrinoma Syndrome (PUD + Diarrhoea + Hypergastrinaemia)
pNET vs CarcinoidpNET = Pancreatic Origin; Carcinoid = GI/Bronchial Origin
Non-Functional pNETNo Hormone Syndrome; Incidental / Mass Effect
Gastrinoma LocationMEN1 = Duodenal (Multiple); Sporadic = Pancreatic
Insulinoma LocalisationEUS (90%) > SACI (Gold Standard Functional)
Glucagonoma TriadNME + Diabetes + Weight Loss
VIPomaWDHA Syndrome (Watery Diarrhoea, Hypokalaemia, Achlorhydria)
SomatostatinomaDiabetes + Steatorrhoea + Gallstones + Hypochlorhydria
PRRT¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE; NETTER-1: PFS 65% vs 11% (Octreotide)
Everolimus/SunitinibRADIANT-3: Everolimus PFS 11 vs 4.6mo; Sunitinib 11.4 vs 5.5mo

13. Mind Map

```mermaid
mindmap

root((Pancreatic NETs))

Grading (WHO 2019)

NET G1: Ki-67 <3%, Mitosis <2

NET G2: Ki-67 3-20%, Mitosis 2-20

NEC G3: Ki-67 >20%, Mitosis >20

```

14. Exam Pearls (FCPS/MRCP)

TopicKey Point
Most Common Functional pNETInsulinoma (40-50%)
Insulinoma Diagnosis72h Fast: Glucose <2.8 + Insulin ≥6 + C-Pep ≥200 + Proinsulin ≥5
Insulinoma TreatmentEnucleation (Curative, 90% Benign)
Gastrinoma (ZES)Fasting Gastrin >1000 pg/mL + pH <2; Secretin Test >120 pg/mL Rise
Gastrinoma LocationDuodenal > Pancreatic (MEN1: Multiple Duodenal)
Glucagonoma TriadNME + Diabetes + Weight Loss
VIPomaWDHA Syndrome: Watery Diarrhoea, Hypokalaemia, Achlorhydria
SomatostatinomaDiabetes + Steatorrhoea + Gallstones + Hypochlorhydria
Non-Functional pNET60-90%; <1cm G1 → Observe; >2cm/G2/G3 → Resect
LocalisationEUS (90-95%) > ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PET (Staging)
Insulinoma LocalisationEUS (90%) > SACI (Selective Arterial Calcium Stimulation)
PRRT (¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE)NETTER-1: PFS 65% vs 11% (Octreotide)
Everolimus/SunitinibRADIANT-3 / NETTER-1: PFS Benefit
CAPTEMCapecitabine + Temozolomide (NEC G3 / High-Grade G2)
SurveillanceG1: Annual; G2: 6-Monthly; G3: 3-Monthly

16. MCQs (10)

  1. Pancreatic NETs (pNETs) classification by function:

A. Functional (hormone syndrome) vs Non-functional (no syndrome)

B. Benign vs Malignant

C. Size-based only

D. Grade-based only

E. Location-based only

  1. Most common functional pNET:

A. Insulinoma (β-cell)

B. Gastrinoma

C. Glucagonoma

D. VIPoma

E. Somatostatinoma

  1. Insulinoma: Whipple's triad + fasting test:

A. Symptoms + glucose <2.8 mmol/L + relief with glucose; 72h fast: glucose <2.8 + insulin ≥6 + C-peptide ≥0.6 + proinsulin ≥5

B. Random glucose only

C. OGTT

D. HbA1c

E. C-peptide only

  1. Gastrinoma (Zollinger-Ellison):

A. Fasting gastrin >1000 pg/mL (or >10x ULN) + pH <2; secretin test: gastrin rise >120 pg/mL

B. Gastrin >100

C. pH >4

D. Secretin negative

E. No diagnostic criteria

  1. Glucagonoma syndrome:

A. Necrolytic migratory erythema + diabetes + weight loss + thrombosis + glossitis

B. Only rash

C. Only diabetes

D. Only weight loss

E. Hypoglycaemia

  1. VIPoma (Verner-Morrison):

A. Watery diarrhoea (secretory, >1L/day) + hypokalaemia + achlorhydria (WDHA syndrome)

B. Constipation

C. Hyperkalaemia

D. Normal stool

E. Only flushing

  1. Somatostatinoma:

A. Diabetes + steatorrhoea + gallstones + hypochlorhydria + weight loss

B. Hypoglycaemia

C. Watery diarrhoea

D. Flushing

E. Only gallstones

  1. Non-functional pNET presentation:

A. Incidental (imaging), mass effect (pain, obstruction), metastatic

B. Hormone syndrome

C. Only acute pancreatitis

D. Only jaundice

E. Only diabetes

  1. pNET grading (WHO 2019):

A. G1: Ki-67 <3%, mitoses <2; G2: Ki-67 3-20% or mitoses 2-20; G3: Ki-67 >20% or mitoses >20

B. G1: Ki-67 <5%

C. G2: Ki-67 5-20%

D. G3: Ki-67 >10%

E. No grading

  1. pNET imaging for localisation:

A. Multiphasic CT/MRI + ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PET/CT (SSTR2+); EUS for small tumours

B. CT only

C. MRI only

D. US only

E. FDG-PET only

17. SBA Questions (5)

  1. 45yo woman: recurrent hypoglycaemia (glucose 2.2) with confusion, relieved by IV dextrose. 72h fast: glucose 2.0, insulin 18, C-peptide 2.5, proinsulin 15. CT: 1.5cm pancreatic tail lesion. Diagnosis?

A. Insulinoma

B. Gastrinoma

C. Glucagonoma

D. Nesidioblastosis

E. Factitious hypoglycaemia

  1. 50yo man: refractory peptic ulcers, diarrhoea, fasting gastrin 1500 pg/mL, pH 1.5. Secretin test: gastrin rise 200 pg/mL. CT: 2cm duodenal mass. Diagnosis?

A. Gastrinoma (Zollinger-Ellison)

B. Insulinoma

C. Glucagonoma

D. VIPoma

E. Non-functional pNET

  1. 60yo woman: migratory necrolytic erythema, new-onset diabetes, weight loss, DVT. Glucagon 800 pg/mL (normal <50). CT: 4cm pancreatic tail mass, liver mets. Management?

A. Surgical resection if resectable + somatostatin analogues for symptom control

B. SST analogues only

C. Chemo only

D. PRRT only

E. Supportive only

  1. Non-functional pNET 3cm, G2 (Ki-67 8%), no mets. Management?

A. Surgical resection (distal pancreatectomy/enucleation) + lymphadenectomy

B. Observation

C. SST analogues

D. PRRT

E. Chemo

  1. Metastatic well-diff pNET G1/G2, SSTR2+, progressive on SST analogues. Next?

A. PRRT (¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE) → everolimus/sunitinib → chemo (CAPTEM)

B. Chemo first

C. Everolimus first

D. Sunitinib first

E. Best supportive care

18. Flashcards

  • Q: pNET classification

A: Functional (insulinoma, gastrinoma, glucagonoma, VIPoma, somatostatinoma) vs Non-functional

  • Q: Insulinoma

A: β-cell; Whipple's triad; 72h fast diagnostic; 90% benign, solitary, intrapancreatic; enucleation

  • Q: Gastrinoma

A: ZES; gastrin >1000 + pH<2; secretin test >120 rise; 60-90% malignant; duodenum > pancreas; MEN1 link

  • Q: Glucagonoma

A: α-cell; NME + diabetes + weight loss + thrombosis + glossitis; glucagon >500; 50-80% metastatic

  • Q: VIPoma

A: WDHA syndrome: Watery Diarrhoea + Hypokalaemia + Achlorhydria; VIP >75 pg/mL; 60% metastatic

  • Q: Somatostatinoma

A: δ-cell: diabetes + steatorrhoea + gallstones + hypochlorhydria; rare; duodenum/pancreas

  • Q: Non-functional

A: No hormone syndrome; incidental or mass effect/metastatic; 60-90% malignant

  • Q: MEN1 link

A: Gastrinoma (duodenal), insulinoma; 30-80% of MEN1 have pNET

  • Q: Grading (WHO 2019)

A: G1: Ki-67<3%, <2 mitoses; G2: Ki-67 3-20% or 2-20 mitoses; G3: Ki-67>20% or >20 mitoses

  • Q: Imaging

A: Multiphasic CT/MRI + 68Ga-DOTATATE PET (SSTR2+); EUS for <1cm; FDG-PET for G3

  • Q: Surgery

A: Enucleation (insulinoma <2cm); distal pancreatectomy (tail); Whipple (head); lymphadenectomy

  • Q: Medical Rx

A: Functional: SST analogues (octreotide/lanreotide); Non-func metastatic: SST → everolimus/sunitinib

  • Q: PRRT

A: 177Lu-DOTATATE for SSTR2+ progressive G1/G2; 7.4 GBq q8wk ×4

  • Q: Chemo

A: CAPTEM (capecitabine + temozolomide) or streptozocin-based for G2/G3

  • Q: Surveillance

A: Func: hormone markers; Non-func: imaging q6-12mo (CT/MRI/DOTATATE)

  1. Multiphasic CT/MRI + ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PET/CT (SSTR2+); EUS for small tumours — pNET imaging for localisation:

SBAs

  1. Gastrinoma (Zollinger-Ellison) — 50yo man: refractory peptic ulcers, diarrhoea, fasting gastrin 1500 pg/mL, pH 1.5. Secretin test: gastrin rise 200 pg/mL. CT: 2cm duodenal mass. Diagnosis?

topics.title: Who 2019 Net Grading
topics.html:
[!info]
WHO 2019 Classification = Unified Grading System for All Neuroendocrine Neoplasms (NENs) Across All Sites. Three-Tier System: NET G1, NET G2, NEC G3. Ki-67 Index and Mitotic Count as Primary Parameters. Well-Differentiated (NET G1/G2) vs Poorly-Differentiated (NEC G3) = Fundamental Prognostic/Treatment Distinction.

1. Learning Objectives

By the end of this note you should be able to:

  • [ ] Apply WHO 2019 grading criteria using Ki-67 index and mitotic count
  • [ ] Distinguish well-differentiated NETs (G1/G2) from poorly-differentiated NEC (G3)
  • [ ] Apply grading to guide prognosis and treatment selection
  • [ ] Recognise site-specific adaptations and exceptions
  • [ ] Recognise mixed neuroendocrine-non-neuroendocrine neoplasms (MiNEN)

2. WHO 2019 Grading Criteria — Three-Tier System

GradeMitotic Count (per 2 mm²)Ki-67 Index (%)TerminologyDifferentiation
NET G1<2<3%Well-Differentiated NET, Grade 1Well-Differentiated
NET G22-203-20%Well-Differentiated NET, Grade 2Well-Differentiated
NEC G3>20>20%Poorly Differentiated Neuroendocrine Carcinoma, Grade 3Poorly Differentiated
Key Principle: Ki-67 Index is Primary Parameter; Mitotic Count Used if Ki-67 Unavailable/Equivocal.

Ki-67 Assessment — Technical Requirements

RequirementSpecification
AntibodyMIB-1 (Standard)
Counting MethodHotspot Method (Highest Labelling Region)
Field Count≥2,000 Tumour Cells (or 10 HPFs at 40x)
ScanningSystematic (Do Not Select Only Hotspots)
ReportingPercentage (Ki-67 Index) + Mitotic Count (per 2mm²)

3. Grade Definitions & Clinical Implications

GradeKi-67Mitoses/2mm²DifferentiationTypical BehaviourMedian Survival
NET G1<3%<2Well-DifferentiatedIndolent; Slow GrowthYears-Decades
NET G23-20%2-20Well-DifferentiatedIntermediate GrowthYears
NEC G3>20%>20Poorly DifferentiatedAggressive; Rapid GrowthMonths (6-12)
Critical Distinction: Well-Differentiated (NET G1/G2) vs Poorly Differentiated (NEC G3) — Fundamental Prognostic & Therapeutic Divide.

4. Ki-67 Assessment — Practical Points

IssueGuidance
Hotspot vs GlobalHotspot Method (Select Region of Highest Labelling)
Cell Count≥2000 Tumour Nuclei (Minimum)
HeterogeneitySample Multiple Blocks (Primary + Metastases)
24h FixationOptimal Fixation: 6-72h in 10% Neutral Buffered Formalin
AntibodyMIB-1 (Standard); Validate with Controls
DiscordanceIf Ki-67 & Mitotic Count Discrepant → Assign Higher Grade

5. Grade-Specific Prognosis & Management

GradePrognosisStandard TreatmentTargeted Therapy
NET G1Excellent (10-20+ yr)Surveillance (<1cm); Surgery (Resectable)SST Analogues (Symptom Control); PRRT (G1/G2 Progressive)
NET G2Good (5-10 yr)Surgery (Resectable); SST Analogues (Symptomatic)PRRT (¹⁷⁷Lu-DOTATATE); Everolimus/Sunitinib
NEC G3Poor (6-18 mo)Platinum/Etoposide Chemotherapy (1st Line)Chemo (Cisplatin/Etoposide); NO PRRT
Critical: Well-Differentiated (NET G1/G2) ≠ NEC G3 — Different Biology, Treatment, Prognosis.

6. Site-Specific Adaptations & Exceptions

SiteSpecific Considerations
Pancreatic NETsStandard WHO 2019 Applies; Insulinoma Often G1; Non-Functional Often G1/G2
Bronchial NETsTypical Carcinoid = G1; Atypical Carcinoid = G2; LCNEC/SCLC = NEC G3
Small Intestine / AppendixMostly G1; Goblet Cell Carcinoma = Separate Entity
Rectal NETsOften G1; <1cm Often Endoscopic Resection; Size <1cm = Low Malignant Potential
Merkel Cell CarcinomaAlways NEC G3 (By Definition); MCPyV+ vs UV-Negative
Thymic NETsOften G2/G3; Paraneoplastic (Myasthenia, Cushing)
Medullary Thyroid CancerAlways G3 (Calitonin High); RET Mutation Testing

7. Mixed Neuroendocrine-Non-Neuroendocrine Neoplasms (MiNEN)

FeatureDetails
DefinitionSingle Tumour with Both Neuroendocrine (NE) + Non-NE Components
CriteriaEach Component ≥30% of Tumour Volume
NE ComponentGraded per WHO 2019 (NET G1/G2 or NEC G3)
Non-NE ComponentGraded per Site-Specific Criteria (e.g., Adenocarcinoma Grading)
Site PredilectionAppendix, Colon, Stomach, Pancreas
PrognosisDriven by Higher-Grade Component
ManagementTreat Both Components (e.g., Surgery + Chemo for NEC Component)

8. Grading & Treatment Algorithm

```

NEN Diagnosed

│

├── WELL-DIFFERENTIATED (Morphology)

│ ├── Ki-67 <3% & Mitoses <2 → NET G1

│ │ → Surgery if Resectable; Surveillance; SST Analogues; PRRT (Progressive)

│ ├── Ki-67 3-20% / Mitoses 2-20 → NET G2

│ │ → Surgery (Resectable); SST Analogues; Everolimus/Sunitinib; PRRT

│ └── Ki-67 >20% / Mitoses >20

│ ├── WELL-DIFFERENTIATED MORPHOLOGY → NET G3 (Rare, Controversial)

│ └── POORLY DIFFERENTIATED MORPHOLOGY → NEC G3

│ → Platinum/Etoposide Chemo; NO PRRT

```

9. Special Categories & Exceptions

NET G3 (Well-Differentiated Grade 3)

FeatureDetails
DefinitionWell-Differentiated Morphology + Ki-67 >20% (or Mitoses >20)
FrequencyRare (~5% of NENs)
BehaviourIntermediate (Better than NEC G3, Worse than NET G2)
ManagementControversial — SST Analogues + PRRT Often Used; Chemo if Aggressive

Mixed Neuroendocrine-Non-Neuroendocrine Neoplasms (MiNEN)

FeatureDetails
DefinitionSingle Tumour with NE + Non-NE Components (Each ≥30%)
GradingNE Component Graded per WHO 2019; Non-NE Graded by Site Rules
Common SitesAppendix, Colon, Stomach, Pancreas
PrognosisDriven by Higher-Grade Component
TreatmentMultimodal (Surgery + Systemic Therapy for Each Component)

Amphicrine / Composite Tumours

FeatureDetails
DefinitionDual Differentiation (Same Cell Lineage: NE + Non-NE) — Rare
ExampleGoblet Cell Carcinoids (Appendix) — Hybrid Features

10. Grading & Treatment Algorithm

Decision Tree for Treatment

```

NEN Diagnosis

│

├── Morphology Well-Differentiated?

│ ├── YES → Ki-67 <3% / Mitoses <2 → NET G1 → Surgery / Surveillance / SST Analogues

│ ├── YES → Ki-67 3-20% / Mitoses 2-20 → NET G2 → Surgery + SST Analogues ± PRRT / Everolimus / Sunitinib

│ └── YES → Ki-67 >20% / Mitoses >20

│ ├── Well-Diff Morphology → NET G3 (Rare) → SST Analogues + PRRT ± Chemo

│ └── Poorly Diff Morphology → NEC G3 → Platinum/Etoposide Chemo (NO PRRT)

│

└── Morphology Poorly Differentiated → NEC G3 → Platinum/Etoposide Chemo

```

11. Grade-Specific Surveillance & Monitoring

GradeImagingBiochemicalFrequency
NET G1Annual CT/MRI + ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATE PETCgA, HormonesAnnual
NET G26-Monthly CT/MRI + ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATECgA, Hormones, Ki-676-Monthly
NET G3 (Well-Diff)3-6 Monthly CT + ⁶⁸Ga-DOTATATECgA, Hormones3-6 Months
NEC G33-Monthly CT + FDG-PETCgA, NSE, ProGRP3-Monthly

12. Treatment Selection by Grade

Grade1st Line2nd Line3rd Line
NET G1Surgery / SurveillanceSST AnaloguesPRRT (Progressive)
NET G2Surgery / SST AnaloguesPRRT / Everolimus / SunitinibChemotherapy (CAPTEM)
NET G3 (Well-Diff)SST Analogues + PRRTEverolimus / SunitinibChemotherapy (If Aggressive)
NEC G3Platinum + EtoposideCAPTEM (Capecitabine + Temozolomide)Clinical Trial

Key Drug Indications by Grade

DrugNET G1NET G2NET G3NEC G3
SST Analogues1st Line (Symptom Control)1st LineConsiderNot Recommended
PRRTProgressive1st Line (Progressive)ConsiderContraindicated
Everolimus2nd Line1st Line2nd LineNo
Sunitinib2nd Line1st Line2nd LineNo
Platinum/EtoposideNoRareConsider1st Line
CAPTEMNo2nd Line2nd Line2nd Line

13. Exam Pearls (FCPS/MRCP)

TopicKey Point
WHO 2019 Grading3 Tiers: NET G1 (<3% Ki-67), NET G2 (3-20%), NEC G3 (>20% Ki-67)
Primary ParameterKi-67 Index (Primary); Mitotic Count (Secondary)
NET G1 vs G2G1: <3% Ki-67; G2: 3-20% Ki-67
NEC G3Poorly Differentiated; Ki-67 >20%; Mitoses >20/2mm²
Well-Diff vs Poorly-DiffFundamental Divide: NET G1/G2 vs NEC G3
NET G3 ControversyWell-Diff Morphology + Ki-67 >20% = "NET G3" (Controversial)
MiNEN≥30% Each Component (NE + Non-NE); Grade Each Separately
Ki-67 AssessmentMIB-1; Hotspot Method; ≥2000 Cells; >20% = G3
NET G3 ControversyWell-Diff Morphology + Ki-67 >20% = "NET G3" (Debated)
NEC G3 TreatmentCisplatin/Etoposide (SCLC-Type); No PRRT
PRRT IndicationSSTR2+ NET G1/G2 Progressive; Not for NEC G3
MiNEN≥30% Each Component; Grade Separately
Ki-67 AssessmentMIB-1; Hotspot; ≥2000 Cells; Discordant → Higher Grade
Mitotic CountPer 2mm²; 40x HPF; 5mm² Field Area

14. Mind Map

```mermaid

mindmap

root((WHO 2019 NET Grading))

Three Tiers

NET G1: Ki-67 <3%, Mitoses <2

NET G2: Ki-67 3-20%, Mitoses 2-20

NEC G3: Ki-67 >20%, Mitoses >20

Key Distinction

NET G1/G2 = Well-Differentiated → Favorable

NEC G3 = Poorly Differentiated → Aggressive

Ki-67 Assessment

MIB-1 Antibody

Hotspot Method (Highest Labeling)

≥2000 Cells Counted

Discordance → Higher Grade

Corner Cases

NET G3 (Well-Diff + Ki-67 >20%)

MiNEN (≥30% NE + Non-NE)

Amphicrine

Treatment by Grade

G1: Surgery/Surveillance/SST

G2: Surgery/SST/PRRT/Everolimus

G3 (Well-Diff): SST/PRRT ± Chemo

NEC G3: Platinum/Etoposide (No PRRT)

MiNEN

≥30% NE + Non-NE

Grade Each Separately

Treat Both Components

```

15. Exam Pearls (FCPS/MRCP)

TopicKey Point
WHO 2019 GradesNET G1 (<3% Ki-67), NET G2 (3-20%), NEC G3 (>20%)
Primary Grading ParameterKi-67 Index (MIB-1, Hotspot, ≥2000 Cells)
NET G1Ki-67 <3%, Mitoses <2; Indolent; Surveillance/Surgery
NET G2Ki-67 3-20%, Mitoses 2-20; Surgery + SST Analogues/PRRT
NEC G3Ki-67 >20%, Mitoses >20; Poorly Differentiated; Platinum/Etoposide
NET G1 vs G2Ki-67 Cutoff at 3%
NEC G3No PRRT; Cisplatin/Etoposide Chemotherapy
Mitotic CountPer 2mm² (40x HPF, ~0.5mm²); 10 HPFs Standard
Ki-67 HotspotHighest Labelling Region; ≥2000 Nuclei
NET G3 ControversyWell-Diff Morph + Ki-67 >20% = "NET G3" (Debated Category)
MiNENNE + Non-NE ≥30% Each; Grade Separately; Treat Both
Ki-67 vs MitosesKi-67 Primary; Mitoses Secondary (If Ki-67 Unavailable)
Discordance RuleKi-67 vs Mitoses Discordant → Assign Higher Grade
MiNENNE + Non-NE Each ≥30%; Grade Each Separately
NEC G3 TreatmentCisplatin + Etoposide (Like SCLC); No PRRT
PRRT EligibilitySSTR2+ NET G1/G2 Progressive; Not NEC G3
MiNEN PrognosisDriven by Higher-Grade Component

16. Local Navigation (for Dashboard UI)

Parent: [[../Neuroendocrine Tumours|Neuroendocrine Tumours]]
Hierarchy: [[../../Davidson Chapter 20 - Endocrinology Hierarchy|Endocrinology Hierarchy]]
Template: [[../../../Templates/Endocrinology Topic Template|Endocrinology Topic Template]]
See also: [[Carcinoid Syndrome]], [[Pancreatic NETs]], [[Bronchial NETs]], [[Merkel Cell Carcinoma]], [[Neuroendocrine Tumours]], [[MEN1]]

17. MCQs (10)

  1. WHO 2019 NET grading uses:

A. Ki-67 index + mitotic count (per 2mm²); both assessed, higher grade assigned

B. Ki-67 only

C. Mitotic count only

D. Size only

E. Stage only

  1. NET G1 (well-differentiated) criteria:

A. Ki-67 <3% AND mitoses <2/2mm²

B. Ki-67 <5%

C. Ki-67 3-20%

D. Ki-67 >20%

E. Any Ki-67 if mitoses <2

  1. NET G2 (well-differentiated) criteria:

A. Ki-67 3-20% OR mitoses 2-20/2mm²

B. Ki-67 <3%

C. Ki-67 >20%

D. Mitoses >20

E. Size >2cm

  1. NEC G3 (poorly differentiated) criteria:

A. Ki-67 >20% OR mitoses >20/2mm² + poorly differentiated morphology

B. Ki-67 3-20%

C. Well-differentiated morphology

D. Mitoses <2

E. Ki-67 <3%

  1. NET G3 (well-differentiated high-grade) — NEW in WHO 2019:

A. Ki-67 >20% OR mitoses >20/2mm² BUT well-differentiated morphology (NET G3 distinct from NEC G3)

B. Poorly differentiated

C. Ki-67 <3%

D. Mitoses <2

E. Not a real category

  1. Ki-67 assessment methodology:

A. Hot spot counting: 500-2000 cells in highest labelling area; MIB-1 antibody

B. Random fields only

C. Average of whole tumour

D. Only mitotic count

E. No standardisation

  1. Mitotic count methodology:

A. 2mm² (10 HPF at 40x); count mitoses in most mitotically active areas

B. 1mm²

C. Whole slide

D. Random fields

E. No standard

  1. Grade determines:

A. Prognosis + treatment (G1/G2: SST/PRRT/everolimus/sunitinib; G3 NEC: platinum+etoposide)

B. Only prognosis

C. Only treatment

D. Neither

E. Surgery only

  1. pNET vs GI-NET grading:

A. Same WHO 2019 criteria apply to all gastroenteropancreatic NETs

B. Different criteria

C. pNET no grading

D. GI-NET no grading

E. Only pNET graded

  1. NET G3 vs NEC G3 treatment difference:

A. NET G3: SST/PRRT/everolimus/sunitinib/CAPTEM; NEC G3: platinum+etoposide (chemo 1st line)

B. Same treatment

C. Both chemo only

D. Both SST only

E. No treatment for either

18. SBA Questions (5)

  1. Pancreatic NET biopsy: Ki-67 25% (hot spot), mitoses 15/2mm², well-differentiated morphology (organoid, trabecular). Grade?

A. NET G3 (well-differentiated high-grade) — NEW category in WHO 2019

B. NEC G3

C. NET G2

D. NET G1

E. Cannot grade

  1. Same patient: treatment approach for metastatic NET G3?

A. CAPTEM or SST analogues ± PRRT (like G2); NOT platinum+etoposide 1st line

B. Platinum + etoposide (like NEC G3)

C. Surgery only

D. Observation

E. Sunitinib only

  1. Small cell lung cancer morphology: Ki-67 80%, mitoses 60/2mm², necrosis, poorly differentiated. Grade?

A. NEC G3 (poorly differentiated neuroendocrine carcinoma)

B. NET G3

C. NET G2

D. NET G1

E. LCNEC

  1. Lung NET: Ki-67 5%, mitoses 3/2mm². Classification?

A. Atypical carcinoid (AC) — intermediate grade NET G2 equivalent

B. Typical carcinoid (TC)

C. LCNEC

D. SCLC

E. NET G1

  1. Ki-67 heterogeneity: biopsy shows 3%, resection shows 18%. Which grade?

A. Grade based on HIGHEST sample (resection 18% = NET G2); biopsy may underestimate

B. Biopsy grade (3% = G1)

C. Average (10% = G2)

D. Cannot determine

E. Always G3

19. Flashcards

  • Q: WHO 2019 grading

A: Ki-67 + mitotic count (2mm²); assign HIGHER grade if discordant

  • Q: NET G1

A: Ki-67 <3% + mitoses <2/2mm²; well-differentiated

  • Q: NET G2

A: Ki-67 3-20% OR mitoses 2-20/2mm²; well-differentiated

  • Q: NET G3 (NEW)

A: Ki-67 >20% OR mitoses >20/2mm² + WELL-DIFFERENTIATED morphology

  • Q: NEC G3

A: Ki-67 >20% OR mitoses >20/2mm² + POORLY DIFFERENTIATED morphology

  • Q: Key distinction

A: NET G3 vs NEC G3 = MORPHOLOGY (well vs poorly differentiated)

  • Q: Ki-67 method

A: MIB-1 antibody; hot spot; 500-2000 cells; report highest

  • Q: Mitotic count

A: 2mm² (10 HPF at 40x); most active areas

  • Q: Lung NET terms

A: TC = NET G1; AC = NET G2; LCNEC = NEC G3; SCLC = NEC G3

  • Q: Treatment by grade

A: G1/G2: SST, PRRT, everolimus, sunitinib, CAPTEM; NEC G3: platinum+etoposide

  • Q: NET G3 treatment

A: Like G2 (SST/PRRT/CAPTEM/targeted); NOT platinum+etoposide 1st line

  • Q: NEC G3 treatment

A: Platinum + etoposide (cisplatin/carboplatin); 1st line chemo

  • Q: Discordance rule

A: Higher grade assigned if Ki-67 and mitoses give different grades

  • Q: Biopsy vs resection

A: Grade based on HIGHEST specimen (resection > biopsy)

  • Q: Clinical impact

A: Grade drives prognosis, surveillance intensity, and therapeutic algorithm
